name: codex
description: Get a second opinion from OpenAI's Codex CLI when stuck on difficult debugging, code analysis, or architecture problems, run a code/diff review (finder and adversarial-refuter passes), run a security review/audit or verification of security-sensitive code (auth, input validation, crypto, secrets), diagnose a bug, hand it a well-scoped edit or clearly-bounded implementation task, generate images with Codex's $imagegen skill, offload rote throwaway work where nothing can go wrong, or run a recon sweep (enumerations, config/wiring locates, traces, pattern sweeps, routine chores). Runs gpt-5.6-sol at xhigh reasoning on the fast service tier (gpt-5.6-luna for rote/bulk and the recon lane, which now defaults to luna; the fast tier is always pinned). The skill runs inline (no context fork), so it is safe to invoke from the main conversation, subagents, and workflows; also ships a codex-wrapper agent for workflow stages that route to codex by agent type. Every invocation runs through the bundled codex-ask executable (on the Bash tool's PATH via the plugin's bin/), which pins the flags and keeps temp files on absolute scratch paths. Requires the `codex` CLI on PATH.
